chunk_text: TITLE: Old and New Tables WORK: Thus Spoke Zarathustra SECTION: Old and New Tables, selected excerpt AUTHOR: Friedrich Nietzsche THEMES: revaluation, creation, nobility, hardness, future SOURCE_TYPE: text When I came unto men, then found I them resting on an old infatuation: all of them thought they had long known what was good and bad for men. This somnolence did I disturb when I taught that no one yet knoweth what is good and bad:⁠-unless it be the creating one! It is he, however, who createth man's goal, and giveth to the earth its meaning and its future: he only effecteth it that aught is good or bad. I taught them all my poetisation and aspiration: to compose and collect into

title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra - Old and New Tables
author: Friedrich Nietzsche
section: Old and New Tables, selected excerpt
safe_use_note: Use for value creation, revaluation, and future-oriented self-command.
chunk_text: its future: he only effecteth it that aught is good or bad. I taught them all my poetisation and aspiration: to compose and collect into unity what is fragment in man, and riddle and fearful chance. The past of man to redeem, and every "It was" to transform, until the Will saith: "But so did I will it! So shall I will it." Here do I sit and wait, old broken tables around me and also new half-written tables. Behold, here is a new table; but where are my brethren who will carry it with me to the valley and into hearts of flesh? Thus demandeth my great love to the remotest ones: be not considerate of thy neighbour! Man

title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra - Old and New Tables

chunk_text: me to the valley and into hearts of flesh? Thus demandeth my great love to the remotest ones: be not considerate of thy neighbour! Man is something that must be surpassed. There are many divers ways and modes of surpassing: see thou thereto! But only a buffoon thinketh: "man can also be overleapt." What thou doest can no one do to thee again. Lo, there is no requital. He who cannot command himself shall obey. And many a one can command himself, but still sorely lacketh self-obedience. To be true⁠-that can few be! And he who can, will not! Least of all, however, can the good be true. The good must crucify him who deviseth his own virtue! That is

title: Thus Spoke Zarathustra - Old and New Tables

section: Old and New Tables, selected excerpt

chunk_text: who can, will not! Least of all, however, can the good be true. The good must crucify him who deviseth his own virtue! That is the truth! The creator, hate they most, him who breaketh the tables and old values, the breaker⁠-him they call the lawbreaker. Therefore, O my brethren, a new nobility is needed, which shall be the adversary of all populace and potentate rule, and shall inscribe anew the word "noble" on new tables. Let it not be your honour henceforth whence ye come, but whither ye go! Your Will and your feet which seek to surpass you⁠-let these be your new honour. O my brethren, I consecrate you and point you to a new nobility: ye shall

chunk_text: which seek to surpass you⁠-let these be your new honour. O my brethren, I consecrate you and point you to a new nobility: ye shall become procreators and cultivators and sowers of the future. Why so soft? O my brethren; thus do I ask you: are ye then not⁠-my brethren? Become hard!
